name: CFSC the Check Cashing Place 3rd & Vermont
description_short: CFSC at 311 S Vermont Ave offers check cashing without a bank account, plus money transfers, bill payments, prepaid cards, and ATM access in Los Angeles.
description_long:
CFSC The Check Cashing Place 3rd & Vermont is a community financial services provider located at 311 S Vermont Ave in Los Angeles, CA. The company operates as a check-cashing storefront serving unbanked and underbanked consumers in the Los Angeles area who need quick access to funds and alternative financial services.

The location offers a comprehensive suite of services beyond basic check cashing. Customers can cash personal and business checks without a bank account (requiring only a photo ID and personal information), purchase money orders, send domestic and international money transfers, pay bills electronically, obtain prepaid debit cards with no credit check required, access ATMs, sell used phones through EcoATM kiosks, exchange foreign currency (80+ countries), purchase transit tickets and MetroCards, and file tax refund services. The company also offers installment loans and prepaid cellular phone services at this location.

The Vermont Ave location distinguishes itself through extended hours of operation (9am-7pm weekdays, 8pm Friday, 6pm Saturday, 5pm Sunday), one-stop convenience for multiple financial tasks, partnerships with major brands like Western Union, Boost, and Yes Debit, and acceptance of multiple government-issued photo IDs. The company explicitly markets the absence of waiting periods for fund clearing and the elimination of monthly account fees as key differentiators from traditional banks.

As a check-cashing operation, CFSC serves a legitimate niche for consumers without traditional banking relationships, though no pricing information, fee schedules, or APRs are disclosed on the website. The company's reliance on check-cashing services suggests its primary customer base consists of unbanked individuals and those seeking fast cash liquidity rather than credit-building products.
address: 311 S Vermont Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90020

company_info.headquarters: 311 S Vermont Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90020

pros: No bank account required to cash checks—only photo ID and personal information needed
Extended hours including evening (until 8pm Friday) and weekend access (open Sunday 10am-5pm)
One-stop convenience: check cashing, bill pay, money transfers, prepaid cards, ATM, and tax refund services at single location
Prepaid debit cards available with no credit check required
Accepts multiple government-issued photo ID types including passports and foreign country IDs
Foreign currency exchange for 80+ countries available at this location
Partnerships with established brands (Western Union, Boost, Yes Debit, EcoATM) providing credibility
Instant fund access without waiting for checks to clear through banking system
cons: No pricing, fee schedule, or cost structure disclosed on website—customers must call (213) 381-3223 to learn actual fees
Check-cashing services inherently target unbanked/underbanked populations, suggesting higher costs than traditional banking
Limited information about installment loan terms, rates, or eligibility requirements
Website contains grammatical error ('maks managing' instead of 'makes managing') suggesting lower operational polish
No disclosure of regulatory oversight, licensing, or consumer protection policies
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked workers who receive physical checks and need immediate cash access
Immigrants and international travelers needing foreign currency exchange and money transfers to 80+ countries
Small business owners needing quick business check cashing without business banking account requirements
Consumers in Los Angeles seeking convenient bill payment and money order services during extended evening/weekend hours
diagnosis: CFSC 3rd & Vermont is best for unbanked Los Angeles residents and workers without traditional bank accounts who need check cashing, money transfers, and bill payment services in one convenient location. The main caveat is that no fee structure or pricing is disclosed online—customers must call to understand actual costs, which are typically higher than traditional banking due to the immediate liquidity and lack of account requirements.
services: Personal check cashing (no bank account required)
Prepaid debit cards (Yes! Debit Account—no credit check)
